pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies this path: 'synthesize your findings into a reference document saved at /home/ga/Desktop/stem_standards_reference.txt'
Task description explicitly states: 'create a new Collection named STEM Standards Review'
Web search confirms https://www.nextgenscience.org/ is the official Next Generation Science Standards website. Task description lists 'Next Generation Science Standards (nextgenscience.org)'
Web search confirms https://corestandards.org/ is the official Common Core State Standards website. Task description lists 'Common Core (corestandards.org)'
Web search confirms https://iste.org/ is the official International Society for Technology in Education website. Task description lists 'ISTE Standards (iste.org)'
Web search confirms https://nces.ed.gov/ is the official National Center for Education Statistics website. Task description lists 'National Center for Education Statistics (nces.ed.gov)'
Web search confirms this is the correct path for Microsoft Edge Collections storage on Linux systems

pi_items.metadata_value: Instructional Coordinator - curriculum standards research and organization
pi_items.verified_value: Instructional Coordinator - curriculum standards research and organization
pi_items.source: Task description states 'You are an instructional coordinator preparing for a STEM curriculum review'. Web search confirms Instructional Coordinators research education standards, develop educational materials, and assess their effectiveness.

privileged_info_summary: This is a STEM curriculum research task where an Instructional Coordinator must research and organize materials from four authoritative educational sources: Next Generation Science Standards (nextgenscience.org), Common Core State Standards (corestandards.org), ISTE Standards for Technology in Education (iste.org), and National Center for Education Statistics (nces.ed.gov). All four domains are real, public-facing websites maintained by legitimate educational organizations. The task requires creating a Microsoft Edge Collection named 'STEM Standards Review' and saving findings to /home/ga/Desktop/stem_standards_reference.txt. The Collections feature stores data in /home/ga/.config/microsoft-edge/Default/Collections on Linux systems.
